description: The outside part or uppermost layer of something; what is immediately visible. 'Leko' names the apparent—the outer face, the visible layer, what first meets perception.
sound_symbolism: The word presents outward. 'Le' begins with light touch, surface met. Then 'ko' defines with clarity, the outer shown.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo leko' (the surface). Related to 'nulo' (deep)—surface and depth as complementary aspects.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Surface is not superficial—appearances matter, even as we see through to depth.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Surfaces carried ornament—the visible plane where decoration lived and beauty appeared.
